mnemonics.title: SELECT Order
mnemonics.content: Father Often Wears Humpy Old Pants (FROM, ORDER BY, WHERE, HAVING, GROUP BY, ORDER BY, SELECT) - But correct: FWGHSO: FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, SELECT, ORDER BY

mnemonics.title: Aggregate Functions
mnemonics.content: Count Sum Avg Min Max - C-S-A-M-M spells 'CSAMM' like 'sample' - these summarize data

mnemonics.title: WHERE vs HAVING
mnemonics.content: WHERe filters ROWS; HAVing filters GROUPS - Remember: ROWS come before GROUPS alphabetically, and WHERE comes before GROUP BY in SQL

acronyms.usage: Commands that modify database data: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
Standard language for managing relational databases
Represents missing or unknown data in database fields
Keyword to eliminate duplicate rows from query results

visualTips.visualization: Draw a funnel: All Data → FROM → WHERE (filter) → GROUP BY (group) → HAVING (filter groups) → SELECT (choose columns) → ORDER BY (sort) → Result
Imagine a form being filled: Table is the form, columns are fields, VALUES are what you write in each field - must match order and data types
Think of find-and-replace in a document: UPDATE tells which document (table), SET is what to change, WHERE is what to find
Picture an empty box with question mark - we don't know what's inside, it's not zero (box) or empty (no box), it's unknown

analogies.analogy: WHERE is like filtering individual items while shopping; HAVING is like filtering categories after grouping items by type
Like a student's roll number - uniquely identifies each record, no duplicates allowed, cannot be NULL
Like adding a new entry in an address book - you specify which fields to fill for that new contact
Like sending a mass email to everyone in your contacts - dangerous, always double-check before executing
